name: ToolEmu
focus: LM-emulated sandbox for discovering high-stakes failures in tool-using agents.
coverage: 36 high-stakes tools and 144 test cases

name: R-Judge
focus: Safety-risk awareness benchmark over agent interaction traces.
coverage: 569 multi-turn records, 27 risk scenarios, 5 app categories, 10 risk types

name: InjecAgent
focus: Indirect prompt injection benchmark for tool-integrated LLM agents.
coverage: 1,054 test cases, 17 user tools, 62 attacker tools

name: AgentDojo
focus: Dynamic environment for evaluating prompt injection attacks and defenses.
coverage: 97 realistic tasks and 629 security test cases

name: Agent-SafetyBench
focus: Comprehensive benchmark for general agent safety evaluation.
coverage: 349 interaction environments, 2,000 test cases, 8 risk categories

name: Agent Security Bench (ASB)
focus: Formalizes and benchmarks attacks and defenses across multiple agent stages.
coverage: 10 scenarios, 10 agents, 400+ tools, 27 attack/defense methods

name: AgentHarm
focus: Measures harmful multi-step behavior of LLM agents under misuse and jailbreaks.
coverage: 110 malicious tasks, 440 with augmentations, 11 harm categories

name: OpenAgentSafety
focus: Real-tool agent safety framework spanning browsers, code execution, filesystems, bash, and messaging.
coverage: 350+ multi-turn multi-user tasks across 8 risk categories

name: WASP
focus: End-to-end benchmark for realistic web-agent prompt injection attacks.
coverage: Realistic web-agent scenarios with partial attack success up to 86%

name: WAInjectBench
focus: Benchmark for prompt-injection detection methods in web agents, including text and image attacks.
coverage: Fine-grained text and image attack/benign datasets for detector evaluation

name: RAS-Eval
focus: Comprehensive security benchmark supporting simulated and real-world tool execution.
coverage: 80 test cases, 3,802 attack tasks, 11 CWE categories

name: OS-Harm
focus: Safety benchmark for computer-use agents built on OSWorld.
coverage: 150 tasks across deliberate misuse, prompt injection, and model misbehavior

name: BAD-ACTS
focus: Adversarial robustness benchmark for multi-agent systems under harmful-action induction.
coverage: 4 agentic systems and 188 harmful-action examples

name: EU-Agent-Bench
focus: Legal-compliance benchmark for measuring illegal behavior of agents under EU law.
coverage: Human-curated legal benchmark with public preview and held-out private test

name: MCPTox
focus: Systematic benchmark for MCP tool poisoning via malicious metadata without execution.
coverage: 45 live MCP servers, 353 tools, 1,312 malicious test cases, 10 risk categories

name: MCP Security Bench (MSB)
focus: End-to-end evaluation of MCP-specific attacks across planning, invocation, and response handling.
coverage: 12 attack types, 10 domains, 405 tools, 2,000 attack instances

name: MCP-SafetyBench
focus: Multi-turn safety benchmark on real MCP servers with cross-server workflows.
coverage: 5 domains and 20 MCP attack types spanning server, host, and user sides

name: MalURLBench
focus: Benchmark for malicious-URL handling failures in web agents.
coverage: 61,845 attack instances, 10 scenarios, 7 malicious-site categories

name: AgentDyn
focus: Dynamic open-ended prompt injection benchmark for real-world agent systems.
coverage: 60 open-ended tasks and 560 injection test cases

name: GAP
focus: Measures divergence between text-level refusal and actual tool-call safety.
coverage: 6 models, 6 regulated domains, 7 jailbreak scenarios, 17,420 datapoints

name: BackdoorAgent
focus: Stage-aware backdoor benchmark covering planning, memory, and tool-use attacks.
coverage: 4 agent applications spanning QA, code, web, and drive

name: SafeAudit
focus: Meta-audit framework for enumerating uncovered unsafe tool-call workflows.
coverage: Audits 3 benchmarks and 12 environments; finds >20% residual unsafe behaviors

name: PoisonBench
focus: Data-poisoning benchmark for preference learning in LLM alignment pipelines.
coverage: 2 attack types, 8 realistic scenarios, 21 models

name: ELBA-Bench
focus: Unified benchmark for LLM backdoor attacks via PEFT or no-finetune methods.
coverage: 1,300+ experiments, 12 attack methods, 18 datasets, 12 LLMs

name: OSCAR Benchmark Dataset
focus: Dynamic benchmark dataset for open-source supply-chain poisoning detection in NPM and PyPI.
coverage: Real-world malicious and benign package benchmark; F1 0.95 on NPM and 0.91 on PyPI

name: QUT-DV25
focus: Dynamic analysis dataset for next-gen PyPI supply-chain attacks.
coverage: 14,271 packages, 7,127 malicious, 36 real-time features

name: SynthChain
focus: Synthetic benchmark and forensic dataset for advanced, stealthy software supply-chain attacks.
coverage: 7 supply-chain scenarios, 0.58M raw events, 1.50M evaluation rows
